video_title: The Twister: Caught in the Storm | Official Trailer | Netflix
reply_text: We get sirens a couple times a week around here. You get to where you don't feel danger from them after so long with no Danger. It begins to feel like an unnecessary hassle to pack up all your kids and all your animals and go climb down a dank set of steps into a musty, dark, cold cellar. The cellar echoes the sounds of all the lightning and thunder, terrifying your kids. Most of the time, the sirens go off for radar indicated rotation... Meaning there's not a tornado on the ground but we get the sirens anyway. Nobody expected it to be as big as it was or that it was even on the ground. Some people thought it was better to stay home... We have been taught that if you don't have a storm cellar, you should seek an interior room with no windows during a tornado, But that doesn't help you when it sucks the roof off your house and destroys every wall. Sirens are great, but we need to know more about storms to better predict them. I watched a tornado put a stick through a car door. You can't always outrun them. Going underground is best, but not everyone has that option. I am one of three houses on my block with a storm cellar. I live 30 minutes from where this tornado hit.
